g.data-narration: Welcome to Regular Stochastic Matrices - a key concept in Markov Chains with real-world applications.
A Stochastic Matrix represents transition probabilities between states in a Markov Chain. Each row sums to 1.
Regular stochastic matrices guarantee that a Markov Chain will converge to a unique steady-state, regardless of initial conditions.
A matrix is Regular if some power P^k has ALL entries strictly greater than zero. This means full state connectivity exists.
g.text: P is regular if ∃ k ≥ 1 such that
"Can reach any state from any other"
Example: P² = P × P may have all positive entries → P is regular!

g.data-narration: Finding P^k helps verify regularity. If all entries become positive after k multiplications, the original matrix is regular.
g.text: 4. Testing for Regularity: The Power Condition
Example: P = [[0.7,0.3],[0.4,0.6]] already has all positive entries → Regular (k=1)

g.data-narration: The steady-state vector π represents long-term equilibrium. It's the probability distribution that remains constant after transitions.
g.text: For ANY initial state v⁽⁰⁾, the system converges to the same π! This is the power of regular matrices.

g.data-narration: To find π, solve π(P-I) = 0 along with Σπᵢ = 1. This gives a unique solution for regular matrices.
Let's apply these concepts to a weather model with Sunny and Rainy states. The transition matrix captures daily weather patterns.
We set up πP = π to find π = [πₛ, πᵣ]. This gives us two equations, one redundant, plus the probability sum constraint.
In the long run, 57.1% of days will be sunny and 42.9% rainy, regardless of today's weather! This is the steady-state.
g.text: Regardless of today's weather, the system converges to these proportions!

g.data-narration: Watch how different starting states converge to the same steady-state over time. This is the hallmark of regular stochastic matrices.
Regular stochastic matrices power real-world applications: Google's PageRank, predictive texting, queueing networks, and genetic algorithms.
Regularity requires two conditions: Irreducible (connected graph) and Aperiodic (no cycles blocking movement).
g.text: 🔗 Irreducible: Path exists from every state to every other state
🔄 Aperiodic: No periodic cycles blocking state returns
If both conditions hold → Transition matrix is Regular!

g.data-narration: In summary: Regular stochastic matrices guarantee convergence to a unique steady-state, making them essential for predicting long-term system behavior.
g.text: ✓ Regular = some Pᵏ has all positive entries
